title: Reconcile the protocol census against the form-definition hierarchy; emit placeholders for unmatched items
description: After §0.A produces the protocol census and §0.B produces each form's base structure, the skill reconciles the two. For each census item whose target is the current form: (1) if the chosen source already includes a field matching this item, use the source's encoding as-is; (2) if another hierarchy level has an encoding even though the chosen source lacks it, carry it forward as a protocol-extension field tagged `library_source: PROTOCOL_EXTENSION`, `completion_status: FLAGGED`; (3) if no hierarchy level provides an encoding, EMIT A PLACEHOLDER FIELD with `library_source: PROTOCOL_INFERRED_PLACEHOLDER`, `completion_status: FLAGGED`, a best-effort generated name (optionally with `_TBD` suffix), the type_hint from the census (text default), and the protocol-implied label verbatim or near-verbatim. Every placeholder also surfaces in `review_flags.placeholders_for_human_completion` with a back-reference to the protocol section and quote that implied it. SILENT OMISSION IS FORBIDDEN — every census item appears in the build, either as a sourced field or as a flagged placeholder.
rationale: Silent omission of a protocol-implied data item is the worst failure mode in a study build. It surfaces months later in monitoring or data review, after enrollment is underway, when reworking the EDC requires costly amendments. The reconciliation step makes the absence visible at build time: any item the protocol asked for that no library source provided becomes a placeholder field a reviewer can see, classify, and complete. The build is auditable end-to-end — every field traces back to either a library source or to a specific protocol quote.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §0.C during Phase B.1d. Advisory — the reconciliation is the protocol-analysis skill's job, executed before the conventions engine sees the spec. Conventions §3, §4, §5, §13, §14 and others further refine placeholder fields (since placeholders are level-3 generated content by definition), so this rule is upstream of those. The placeholder pattern (`library_source: PROTOCOL_INFERRED_PLACEHOLDER`, `completion_status: FLAGGED`, `_TBD` name suffix) is consumed by downstream conventions and by the human-review workflow.
